title: Environment Map
description: Canonical per-environment infrastructure-posture map. Composes with infrastructure-catalog.schema.json /definitions/environment for identity (references by id, does not duplicate) and adds the infra-posture layer that the identity definition's additionalProperties:false structurally cannot carry: tag convention, load-balancer membership mode, firewall membership + SSH posture, held-key requirement, TLS origin-cert provenance, and the canonical per-environment Terraform variable contract. Authored from the env-parity convention substrate proven by d-2026-06-13-eb-dev-droplet-rebuild (dev = compliant reference instance) and verified across dev/staging/prod TF. Honest parity: the schema encodes known exceptions (e.g. production standing-restricted SSH for blue-green) rather than pretending uniformity.

version.description: Semantic version of this environment-map instance.

lastUpdated.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of last instance update.

catalogRef.description: Path or id of the infrastructure-catalog instance whose /definitions/environment entries this map composes with.

environments.description: Per-environment posture entries. Each composes with an infrastructure-catalog environment by environmentRef.

transitions.description: Audit trail of changes to this map.

environmentRef.description: References an infrastructure-catalog /definitions/environment id. Identity (name, url, servers, objectStorage) lives there; this entry adds posture only.

type.description: Mirror of the referenced environment type, for self-contained validation. MUST equal the catalog entry's type.

complianceStatus.description: compliant = meets full canonical posture; convergence-target = known parity work outstanding; exception-declared = deviates by documented design (see firewall.sshPosture standing-restricted).

required.description: Minimum required tag set. MUST include environment:{env} and managed-by:terraform.

descriptors.description: Optional role/topology descriptor tags (product:*, role:*, vpc-isolated, blue-green, dedicated-cpu, blue/green color).

membership.description: LB membership MUST be tag-based (droplet_tag=environment:{env}); droplet_ids membership is a parity violation.
Firewall membership MUST be tag-based (tags=[environment:{env}]), self-healing on droplet replacement; droplet_ids is a parity violation.

sshPosture.description: zero-ssh = no static port-22 inbound, break-glass via admin_ssh_cidrs dynamic open->ssh->close; break-glass-only = same with no zero-ssh steady claim; standing-restricted = standing SSH from fixed sourceAddresses (production blue-green cutover exception).

breakGlassVar.description: Terraform variable name carrying the dynamic break-glass inbound CIDR list (e.g. admin_ssh_cidrs), empty default.

sourceAddresses.description: Required when sshPosture is standing-restricted: the documented fixed source IPs permitted standing SSH.

exceptionRationale.description: Required when sshPosture is standing-restricted: why the exception exists (honest parity, do not hide).

sshKeys.description: Each ssh_key on the env droplet. Every entry MUST reference a resource-access.json credentialStore itemId and be held:true (private half vault-backed, round-trip-verifiable). An unheld key is a parity violation the schema makes detectable.

credentialRef.description: resource-access.json credentialStore itemId (BW item UUID identifier, never the value).

held.description: true iff the private half is vault-backed and retrievable. false marks a known parity violation pending convergence.

deprecated.description: true marks a key scheduled for removal (e.g. unheld luhtech-admin-1 on staging/prod).

leaf.description: GH env secret name for the cert leaf (e.g. SSL_CERT_LEAF_DEVELOPMENT).

privateKey.description: GH env secret name for the cert private key (e.g. SSL_CERT_PRIVATE_KEY_DEVELOPMENT).

bwBacked.description: MUST be true: cert material is BW-backed so a droplet rebuild never silently drops it (dev nearly lost this in the 2026-06-13 rebuild).

requiredTfVars.description: Canonical per-environment Terraform variable contract. Governs the setup-terraform-vars composite action's required-input set (ties to FU-DRIFT-DETECTION-PR-A-HALF-2). Divergence here is a detectable parity gap.

then.$comment: An environment carrying any unheld ssh key cannot be complianceStatus:compliant — it is a convergence-target or exception-declared.
